description_long: This is a shallow, round ceramic bowl with a wide, gently sloping rim and a relatively flat base. The exterior and interior surfaces are predominantly a pale pinkish color, while a significant portion of the interior and part of the rim are covered in a glossy reddish-brown slip or glaze, possibly indicating restoration or a partially preserved original finish. The bowl shows clear evidence of breakage and repair, with visible seams and color differences where fragments have been reassembled. The surface appears smooth and hard, with a subtle sheen on the glazed areas and a more matte finish on the unglazed portions. The bowl is of modest size, suitable for serving or holding small quantities of food or liquid, and is likely hand-crafted, as suggested by the irregularities in shape and finish.
description: A shallow, round ceramic bowl with a pale pink body and a glossy reddish-brown glazed interior, showing visible repairs and color variation.
description_view_0: The bowl is seen from an oblique angle, highlighting the reddish-brown glazed area and visible repair seams on the left side.
description_view_1: A front-facing view emphasizes the wide opening, the extent of the reddish-brown glaze, and the repaired section at the top.
description_view_2: This angle shows more of the unglazed pale pink surface, with the reddish-brown glaze receding to the right and visible repair lines.
description_view_3: The rear view displays mostly the pale pink exterior with a smaller section of reddish-brown glaze and less visible repair work.
